phenotypes.label: Metabolic syndrome cluster
phenotypes.description: This cluster is dominated by metabolic syndrome with associated obesity, hypertension, dyslipidemia, and sleep apnea. Accompanying symptoms such as fatigue and pain reflect the systemic burden and comorbid conditions commonly seen in this population. The pattern represents patients with cardiometabolic risk and multisystem symptom burden centered on metabolic syndrome.

phenotypes.label: Aneurysm-focused cohort
phenotypes.description: This cluster is dominated by aneurysm, particularly thoracic aortic ectasia, with accompanying cardiovascular risk factors such as hypertension and hyperlipidemia. The surrounding conditions reflect typical comorbidity and surveillance findings in patients with known or suspected aneurysmal disease. Overall it represents patients centered on aneurysm diagnosis and related vascular risk context.

phenotypes.label: Prostatic hyperplasia with obstruction
phenotypes.description: This cluster reflects benign prostatic hyperplasia with associated lower urinary tract symptoms and obstruction, including nocturia, urinary frequency, retention, and elevated PSA. It also captures related male genitourinary conditions such as erectile dysfunction and testicular hypofunction, consistent with older men with prostatic enlargement. The pattern represents symptomatic bladder outlet obstruction and its common sequelae.

phenotypes.label: GERD with esophagitis
phenotypes.description: This cluster is dominated by gastroesophageal reflux disease with esophagitis, which overwhelmingly defines the patient profile. Other conditions appear sporadically and do not form a consistent secondary pattern, reflecting incidental comorbidities rather than a unified syndrome. The topic effectively captures patients whose clinical picture is centered on severe or symptomatic reflux disease.

phenotypes.label: Metastatic melanoma with lung involvement
phenotypes.description: This pattern reflects patients with malignant melanoma and prominent pulmonary findings such as lung nodules and abnormal chest imaging, consistent with metastatic spread to the lungs. Additional involvement of kidney, pancreas, and liver conditions supports disseminated malignancy across multiple organ systems. The cluster captures advanced cancer with multi-organ metastases, centered on melanoma with lung involvement.

phenotypes.label: Autoimmune hypothyroidism syndrome
phenotypes.description: This pattern reflects autoimmune hypothyroidism, commonly Hashimoto thyroiditis, with classic features of thyroid atrophy and persistent hypothyroid states. It clusters with nutritional deficiencies (vitamin B12, vitamin D), fatigue, anemia, and reduced bone density, consistent with metabolic and autoimmune sequelae. The enrichment of menopause and osteoporosis suggests a predominance in older women with endocrine and skeletal complications.

phenotypes.label: PTSD, depression, substance use
phenotypes.description: This cluster represents patients with severe psychiatric illness centered on post-traumatic stress disorder, recurrent major depression, and anxiety disorders, with prominent comorbid substance use disorders including alcohol, opioids, and stimulants. The pattern includes features of high clinical acuity such as suicidality, personality disorders, and insomnia, reflecting complex behavioral health needs. Associated findings like dental disease and nicotine dependence are consistent with chronic substance use and psychosocial impairment.

phenotypes.label: Unstructured mixed baseline conditions
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are a grab bag of common chronic diseases, symptoms, and unrelated rare findings without a unifying clinical pattern. High-frequency items reflect general population comorbidity, while the distinguishing items are scattered across unrelated systems. This does not represent a coherent patient phenotype.

phenotypes.label: ADHD with childhood mood disorders
phenotypes.description: This pattern centers on inattentive-type ADHD co-occurring with childhood emotional disorders and persistent depressive symptoms. It reflects a pediatric and adolescent mental health profile with overlapping neurodevelopmental and mood disorders, often accompanied by anxiety and episodic mood instability. The broader list includes typical comorbid psychiatric conditions seen in this population.

phenotypes.label: Degenerative spine and chronic pain
phenotypes.description: This cluster reflects chronic musculoskeletal pain centered on degenerative spine disease, including lumbar spondylosis, disc degeneration, spinal stenosis, and radiculopathy. It extends to regional joint pain (hip, knee, shoulder) and overlapping chronic pain syndromes such as fibromyalgia. Patients typically have multi-site axial and appendicular pain driven by degenerative and neuropathic mechanisms.

phenotypes.label: GI gas with neurovascular mix
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine prominent gastrointestinal gas-related symptoms (abdominal distension, flatulence, stool changes, constipation/IBS) with unrelated vascular and neuropsychiatric features such as arterial disorders, amnesia, and psychotic symptoms. These do not share a single pathophysiologic pathway or organ system. The topic reflects a conflation of gastrointestinal symptom clusters with cerebrovascular or neuropsychiatric conditions rather than a coherent clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Valvular heart disease syndrome
phenotypes.description: This cluster reflects structural valvular heart disease, dominated by mitral valve prolapse and other mitral and aortic valve disorders, with downstream features such as pulmonary hypertension, cardiomegaly, and heart murmurs. Associated symptoms include dyspnea, palpitations, and chest discomfort. The co-occurrence of scoliosis and bone-related conditions suggests an underlying connective tissue phenotype seen in patients with multisystem involvement.

phenotypes.label: Renal stones and ocular disorders
phenotypes.description: This grouping combines kidney stone disease with a cluster of age-related ocular conditions such as macular degeneration, cataract, and refractive disorders, alongside scattered musculoskeletal and systemic conditions. These do not share a single unifying pathophysiologic pathway and represent unrelated clinical domains. The mixture suggests two or more distinct patient patterns captured together rather than a coherent syndrome.

phenotypes.label: Ischemic stroke and TIA
phenotypes.description: This pattern represents acute ischemic cerebrovascular disease, dominated by cerebral infarction and transient ischemic attacks. Associated neurologic deficits such as dysarthria, slurred speech, and ataxia reflect focal brain injury. The presence of vascular risk factors like hyperlipidemia and hypertension supports an atherosclerotic stroke mechanism.

phenotypes.label: Hip enthesopathy
phenotypes.description: This pattern is dominated by hip enthesopathy, reflecting focal musculoskeletal tendon and ligament insertion disorders around the hip. The remaining conditions are heterogeneous and do not form a coherent clinical syndrome, indicating the topic primarily identifies patients with this localized musculoskeletal condition.

phenotypes.label: Breast cancer diagnosis and sequelae
phenotypes.description: This cluster represents breast cancer and its clinical context, including abnormal imaging, palpable masses, and genetic risk. It also captures related features such as lymphedema, postoperative states, and bone involvement consistent with metastasis or treatment effects. The pattern reflects patients undergoing diagnosis and survivorship care for breast malignancy.

phenotypes.label: Lower extremity venous insufficiency
phenotypes.description: Chronic venous and lymphatic disorders of the lower extremities characterized by venous insufficiency, varicose veins, and limb edema. Complications include lymphedema, cellulitis, and episodes of deep vein thrombosis with associated limb pain and swelling. This pattern reflects patients with impaired venous return and chronic lower limb congestion.

phenotypes.label: Glaucoma and cataract spectrum
phenotypes.description: This cluster represents age-related ocular disease, dominated by glaucoma (including suspects and ocular hypertension) and multiple forms of cataract. It also includes associated degenerative and surface eye conditions such as vitreous degeneration, epiretinal membrane, blepharitis, and tear film insufficiency, along with visual impairment. The pattern reflects patients with chronic ophthalmologic disease burden and progressive vision loss.

phenotypes.label: Pregnancy and prenatal complications
phenotypes.description: This cluster represents pregnancy across all trimesters, including high-risk and complicated pregnancies. It includes common pregnancy-associated symptoms and comorbidities such as anemia, obesity, pelvic pain, and genitourinary conditions. The pattern reflects routine and complicated prenatal care populations.

phenotypes.label: Seizure and movement disorders
phenotypes.description: This cluster represents central nervous system disorders characterized by seizures, epilepsy, and movement abnormalities including Parkinsonian features and tremor. It also includes cognitive impairment, dysarthria, and other neurobehavioral changes, reflecting diffuse neurologic dysfunction. Patients typically have chronic neurologic disease with both motor and cognitive manifestations.

phenotypes.label: Baseline comorbidity mix
phenotypes.description: This grouping is dominated by very common chronic conditions and nonspecific symptoms such as hypertension, diabetes, reflux, pain, and mood disorders, without a unifying clinical pattern. The additional conditions that appear more specific are scattered across unrelated organ systems and do not form a coherent syndrome. Overall, it reflects a general baseline mix of comorbidities rather than a distinct patient phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Psychiatric and endocrine mix
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine severe mood disorder (bipolar disorder) with endocrine abnormalities centered on calcium metabolism (hyperparathyroidism, hypercalcemia) and thyroid nodular disease, along with gallstones and assorted unrelated conditions. These do not form a single clinical pathway or syndrome, indicating two or more unrelated patient patterns grouped together. This mixture limits clinical interpretability as a coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Psychiatric crises with acute illness
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine substance use and severe psychiatric symptoms (including suicidal or homicidal ideation, hallucinations, and mood disorders) with acute medical problems such as pneumothorax, pleural effusion, pulmonary edema, and neurologic emergencies. These represent distinct clinical domains without a single unifying disease process. The pattern reflects co-occurrence of behavioral health crises and serious acute medical conditions rather than one coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Neuropathy and endocrine mixed cluster
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine peripheral neuropathies (mononeuritis, polyneuropathy, carpal tunnel) and arthropathy with thyroid disorders and scattered unrelated findings such as carotid disease and malignancy. These do not form a single clinical pathway or syndrome and instead reflect co-occurring but mechanistically distinct problems. The pattern lacks a unifying diagnosis linking neurologic, musculoskeletal, and endocrine features.

phenotypes.label: General medical baseline mix
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are a grab bag of common chronic diseases, nonspecific symptoms, and unrelated findings without a unifying clinical pattern. Highly prevalent conditions like hypertension, hyperlipidemia, pain syndromes, gastrointestinal symptoms, and mood disorders dominate, with scattered dermatologic and other low-frequency findings. This reflects a baseline mix of general medical problems rather than a distinct patient phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Chronic disease baseline mix
phenotypes.description: This cluster reflects the general chronic disease and symptom baseline seen across many patients, dominated by common conditions such as hypertension, diabetes, hyperlipidemia, obesity, and nonspecific pain complaints. The additional conditions are scattered across unrelated systems without a unifying clinical pattern. It does not represent a distinct or clinically coherent patient group.

phenotypes.label: Erectile dysfunction focus
phenotypes.description: Erectile dysfunction overwhelmingly dominates this pattern, with minor co-occurrence of ocular conditions and hypogonadism. The cluster reflects men presenting for sexual dysfunction, often alongside age-related visual disorders and occasional endocrine or metabolic comorbidity. The non-dominant conditions do not form a separate cohesive syndrome.

phenotypes.label: Severe ocular disease and vision loss
phenotypes.description: This pattern reflects advanced ocular disease with vision-threatening complications, including retinal detachment, glaucoma, and diabetic retinopathy leading to bilateral blindness. Associated findings such as cataract, vitreous pathology, and dry eye conditions further indicate chronic, progressive eye disease. The cluster is typical of patients with severe visual impairment, often in the context of diabetes or age-related degeneration.

phenotypes.label: Neurogenic bladder with UTI complications
phenotypes.description: This cluster reflects lower urinary tract dysfunction complicated by infection, with prominent neurogenic bladder, urinary retention, and recurrent cystitis. Associated findings include hematuria, hydronephrosis, and progression to urosepsis and acute kidney injury in more severe cases. The pattern is typical of patients with impaired bladder emptying leading to obstructive and infectious urologic complications.

phenotypes.label: Advanced malignancy with complications
phenotypes.description: This cluster reflects patients with advanced or metastatic malignancy, characterized by pleural and pericardial effusions, lung nodules, hepatomegaly, and bowel obstruction. The prominence of postoperative state and acquired absence of organ suggests prior cancer surgeries with ongoing complications or recurrence. Associated hydronephrosis and widespread imaging abnormalities indicate multisystem involvement typical of late-stage disease.

phenotypes.label: Mixed metabolic, surgical, sensory disorders
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions span disparate domains including lipid metabolism disorders, coagulation abnormalities, abdominal wall hernias and intra-abdominal masses, renal malignancy, and auditory disorders such as hearing loss and tinnitus. These do not align along a single organ system or shared pathophysiologic pathway. The grouping reflects unrelated clinical themes rather than a cohesive phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Advanced cardiometabolic cardiovascular disease
phenotypes.description: This pattern reflects cardiometabolic disease with established cardiovascular and renal complications. Core features include hypertension, type 2 diabetes, and dyslipidemia alongside coronary atherosclerosis, atrial fibrillation, heart failure, and diabetic kidney disease. It represents patients with advanced end-organ involvement from metabolic and vascular disease.

phenotypes.label: Cirrhosis with portal hypertension complications
phenotypes.description: This cluster represents advanced chronic liver disease, centered on cirrhosis from hepatitis C and nonalcoholic steatohepatitis. It includes complications of portal hypertension and hepatic dysfunction such as ascites, splenomegaly, thrombocytopenia, and pancytopenia, along with post–liver transplant status. The pattern reflects decompensated cirrhosis with hematologic and systemic sequelae.

phenotypes.label: Dysphonia / voice disorder
phenotypes.description: This pattern is dominated by dysphonia, indicating patients with primary voice disorders or hoarseness, often accompanied by dysphagia and upper aerodigestive symptoms such as reflux and cough. The presence of post-procedural complications suggests some cases relate to prior airway or neck interventions affecting vocal function. Comorbid cardiopulmonary and gastrointestinal conditions appear as secondary background features.

phenotypes.label: Rheumatoid arthritis systemic complications
phenotypes.description: This cluster centers on rheumatoid arthritis, particularly seropositive disease, with systemic autoimmune features. Associated conditions include extra-articular manifestations such as Sjögren’s syndrome, oral ulcers, alopecia, and hematologic abnormalities, along with increased risk of venous thromboembolism. Bone loss and osteoporosis reflect chronic inflammation and disease burden affecting skeletal health.

phenotypes.label: MS and ADHD mixture
phenotypes.description: This grouping combines two distinct clinical patterns: demyelinating neurologic disease with features such as multiple sclerosis, ataxia, and involuntary movements, alongside neurodevelopmental and psychiatric conditions including ADHD, anxiety, and mood disorders. These conditions do not share a single unifying pathophysiologic mechanism and represent separate patient populations captured together.

phenotypes.label: Distal extremity musculoskeletal disorders
phenotypes.description: This cluster represents musculoskeletal conditions affecting the distal extremities, particularly the feet and hands. It includes osteoarthritis, deformities such as hallux valgus and flatfoot, and soft tissue disorders like plantar fasciopathy, enthesopathy, and tenosynovitis, along with related pain syndromes. The pattern reflects chronic degenerative and overuse conditions of peripheral joints and supporting structures.

phenotypes.label: HIV with opportunistic infections
phenotypes.description: This cluster represents patients with HIV infection and associated opportunistic or co-infections, particularly herpesvirus disease including zoster. Accompanying findings include dermatologic conditions, ear and nasal infections, anemia, and other systemic comorbidities consistent with immunocompromise. The pattern reflects chronic HIV disease with infectious and inflammatory complications.

phenotypes.label: Pancytopenia with bleeding complications
phenotypes.description: This pattern reflects bone marrow failure or hematologic disease characterized by pancytopenia with prominent thrombocytopenia and bleeding manifestations such as epistaxis. Associated features include fever, diarrhea, rash, and immune dysfunction, consistent with infection susceptibility in immunocompromised states. The cluster captures patients with systemic cytopenias and related complications.

phenotypes.label: Nonspecific multimorbidity baseline
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are a broad mix of common chronic diseases, nonspecific symptoms, and scattered organ-specific findings without a unifying clinical theme. Highly prevalent conditions like hypertension, diabetes, and GERD appear alongside unrelated gastrointestinal, cardiopulmonary, and transplant-related entries. This pattern reflects a generic baseline of multimorbidity rather than a distinct clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Mixed neurologic and unrelated conditions
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions mix central neurologic disease (brain disorders, hydrocephalus, seizures, dysarthria) and postoperative states with nonspecific symptoms, alongside unrelated findings such as bunion and endometriosis. These elements do not form a single coherent neurologic or systemic syndrome and instead reflect multiple unrelated clinical themes grouped together. This pattern is not clinically interpretable as a unified phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Cystic fibrosis multisystem disease
phenotypes.description: This pattern reflects cystic fibrosis with multisystem involvement, including bronchiectasis, chronic sinusitis, and recurrent pulmonary infections with hypoxemia. Pancreatic disease with secondary diabetes is prominent, consistent with exocrine insufficiency and endocrine complications. The combination of respiratory and pancreatic manifestations defines a classic CF phenotype.

phenotypes.label: General comorbidity baseline
phenotypes.description: This grouping reflects a broad mix of common chronic conditions (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, obesity, diabetes, mood disorders) alongside nonspecific gastrointestinal complaints and diagnoses. There is no single unifying clinical syndrome; instead it mirrors a general primary care population with frequent comorbidities and intermittent abdominal symptoms. The pattern does not isolate a distinct or clinically actionable phenotype.

phenotypes.label: HIV with COPD and comorbidities
phenotypes.description: This pattern reflects patients with HIV infection and chronic lung disease, particularly COPD, often accompanied by coexisting immunodeficiency and hepatitis C. The cluster also includes substance use (notably cocaine) and serious mental illness, consistent with shared risk factors and social determinants in this population. It represents a medically and socially complex group with overlapping infectious, pulmonary, and behavioral health conditions.

phenotypes.label: Recurrent depression with anxiety
phenotypes.description: This cluster represents patients with recurrent major depressive disorder across varying severities and remission states, frequently co-occurring with generalized anxiety disorder. It captures the longitudinal course of mood disorders, including active episodes and remission, reflecting chronic relapsing depression with comorbid anxiety. Other conditions are minor and do not define the core pattern.

phenotypes.label: Ulcerative colitis with GI bleeding
phenotypes.description: This pattern represents inflammatory bowel disease, dominated by ulcerative colitis with active colonic inflammation. It is characterized by lower gastrointestinal bleeding (hematochezia, melena), diarrhea, and rectal hemorrhage, often leading to iron deficiency anemia from chronic blood loss. Associated findings include abdominal pain and colonic pathology such as polyps and hemorrhoids in the context of inflamed mucosa.

phenotypes.label: Postprocedural complications with mixed findings
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions center on postoperative and postprocedural states with complications and abnormal imaging, but are intermixed with unrelated findings across multiple organ systems (gastrointestinal, genitourinary, cardiovascular, and gynecologic). There is no single unifying disease process linking these conditions, reflecting a heterogeneous grouping of perioperative issues and incidental findings.

phenotypes.label: Knee osteoarthritis syndrome
phenotypes.description: This pattern reflects degenerative knee joint disease, dominated by osteoarthritis with associated pain, effusion, and meniscal injury. The clustering of knee-specific diagnoses and symptoms indicates chronic mechanical joint degeneration, often accompanied by functional limitation. Common comorbidities like obesity and hypertension appear as background but are not the primary driver.

phenotypes.label: Crohn's disease with complications
phenotypes.description: This pattern reflects inflammatory bowel disease, particularly Crohn’s disease, with complications such as abscess formation, bowel inflammation, and gastrointestinal symptoms including diarrhea, abdominal pain, and bleeding. The presence of immunosuppression and anemia suggests chronic, active disease with systemic effects. Associated anorectal conditions and bowel dysfunction point to complicated or fistulizing disease involvement.

phenotypes.label: Dysarthria with brainstem features
phenotypes.description: This pattern is dominated by dysarthria with associated bulbar and brainstem features including dysphagia, nystagmus, diplopia, tremor, and gait instability. It reflects patients with focal neurologic impairment affecting speech and coordination, often involving cerebellar or brainstem pathways. The surrounding conditions are consistent with neuromuscular weakness and impaired cranial nerve function.

phenotypes.label: Sun-related skin lesions
phenotypes.description: This cluster reflects benign and premalignant cutaneous lesions associated with chronic sun exposure and aging, including actinic keratoses, seborrheic keratoses, nevi, and other skin neoplasms. The presence of pigmentation disorders and photoaging changes supports a pattern of cumulative ultraviolet damage. Co-occurring minor ocular aging conditions likely reflect an older patient population rather than a separate disease process.

phenotypes.label: Thyroid disorders and thyrotoxicosis
phenotypes.description: This cluster reflects thyroid disease, dominated by thyrotoxicosis, hypothyroidism, and nodular goiter. The associated features include cardiovascular effects, menstrual disturbance such as amenorrhea, neuropsychiatric symptoms, and metabolic complications, consistent with systemic consequences of thyroid hormone imbalance. Patients in this group show a spectrum of endocrine dysfunction with multisystem involvement.

phenotypes.label: Shoulder disorders and unrelated diseases
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions center on shoulder pathology—rotator cuff disease, impingement, bursitis, and osteoarthritis—alongside chronic musculoskeletal pain. Intermixed are unrelated gastrointestinal diseases and multiple malignancies, including Crohn’s disease and cancers of the lung, kidney, and colon, without a unifying clinical pathway. This combination does not represent a single coherent patient phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Schizophrenia spectrum disorders
phenotypes.description: Severe chronic psychotic illness characterized by schizophrenia and schizoaffective disorder, with related psychotic and mood features. Patients commonly carry cardiometabolic comorbidities such as hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and diabetes, along with neurologic or sensory issues like movement abnormalities, hearing loss, and dizziness. The pattern reflects a population with persistent psychiatric disease and substantial general medical burden.

phenotypes.label: Ocular disease with unrelated metadata
phenotypes.description: This grouping combines ophthalmologic disorders—vitreous degeneration, retinal disease, cataracts, glaucoma risk, and visual disturbance—with sarcoidosis, suggesting an ocular inflammatory phenotype. However, it is intermixed with sexual activity status documentation and unrelated metabolic findings, which do not share a clinical mechanism with the eye disease cluster. The lack of a single coherent clinical story across these elements makes the grouping unreliable for interpretation.

phenotypes.label: Fibrocystic breast disease
phenotypes.description: This topic is dominated by fibrocystic breast disease, a benign breast condition often associated with breast pain, nodularity, and abnormal mammographic findings. Other conditions appear as scattered comorbidities without a unifying clinical pattern, but the central signal is clearly breast-related benign disease in a predominantly female population.

phenotypes.label: Dementia with neuropsychiatric complications
phenotypes.description: Dementia overwhelmingly dominates this pattern, with accompanying cognitive impairment features such as amnesia, attentional deficits, and organic mental disorders. Co-occurring neuropsychiatric symptoms (anxiety, bipolar disorder, suicidal or homicidal ideation) and neurodegenerative conditions like Parkinson’s disease reflect behavioral and cognitive complications in advanced disease. Additional medical events such as pneumonia or thrombosis are consistent with frailty and late-stage functional decline.

phenotypes.label: Sleep apnea syndrome
phenotypes.description: This cluster is dominated by sleep apnea, with associated features such as headaches, fatigue, and hypertension reflecting the systemic effects of disordered sleep and intermittent hypoxia. Co-occurring pain syndromes and neurologic symptoms likely reflect comorbid conditions commonly seen in these patients. The pattern represents patients whose clinical profile is centered on obstructive sleep apnea and its sequelae.

phenotypes.label: Thyroid cancer and thyroidectomy sequelae
phenotypes.description: This cluster represents patients with thyroid neoplasms and nodular thyroid disease, often following thyroidectomy. It includes expected postoperative sequelae such as hypothyroidism and hypocalcemia, along with local compressive or nerve-related symptoms like dysphonia and dysphagia. Associated lymphadenopathy and imaging abnormalities reflect cancer staging and surveillance.

phenotypes.label: Reproductive and general symptom mix
phenotypes.description: This grouping combines a strong cluster of female reproductive and menstrual disorders (irregular bleeding, vaginitis, endometriosis, ovarian conditions) with common mental health conditions and nonspecific primary care complaints such as asthma, pain syndromes, and upper respiratory infections. These represent distinct clinical domains without a single unifying mechanism, indicating two unrelated patient patterns appearing together.

phenotypes.label: Postmenopausal age-related conditions
phenotypes.description: This pattern reflects postmenopausal women with age-related conditions and symptoms. It includes menopausal manifestations (vasomotor symptoms, postmenopausal bleeding, dyspareunia) alongside common midlife findings such as colon polyps, breast masses, and sensorineural hearing loss with tinnitus. The cluster captures a preventive and degenerative health profile typical of aging women.

phenotypes.label: Dermatologic and mood disorders
phenotypes.description: This pattern combines prominent dermatologic conditions (acne, acne vulgaris, inflammatory dermatoses) with mood and anxiety disorders including bipolar II disorder, depression, and generalized anxiety. These represent two distinct clinical domains—skin disease and psychiatric illness—without a unifying pathophysiologic process. The co-occurrence suggests a heterogeneous patient group rather than a single clinical syndrome.

phenotypes.label: Allergic rhinitis with asthma
phenotypes.description: This pattern reflects atopic airway disease with coexisting allergic rhinitis, chronic sinusitis, and asthma across severity levels. The clustering of nasal obstruction, rhinorrhea, and sinus disease with bronchial hyperreactivity is consistent with a unified airway inflammatory process. Patients typically have recurrent upper and lower airway symptoms driven by allergic sensitization.

phenotypes.label: Female pelvic floor dysfunction
phenotypes.description: This pattern reflects lower urinary tract dysfunction in women, dominated by stress, urge, and mixed urinary incontinence with overactive bladder symptoms such as urgency, frequency, and nocturia. Co-occurring atrophic vaginitis, dyspareunia, and postmenopausal state point to genitourinary syndrome of menopause contributing to symptoms. The inclusion of fecal incontinence and pelvic pain suggests broader pelvic floor dysfunction affecting both urinary and bowel continence.

phenotypes.label: Advanced CKD and kidney transplant
phenotypes.description: Advanced chronic kidney disease including end-stage renal disease and kidney transplant recipients, with associated complications. The pattern includes anemia of CKD, secondary hyperparathyroidism, electrolyte disturbances, and proteinuria, along with immunosuppression and immune dysfunction related to transplantation. This represents patients with severe renal impairment and its systemic consequences.

phenotypes.label: Sleep apnea with hypersomnia
phenotypes.description: This pattern is dominated by sleep disorders, particularly obstructive sleep apnea, hypersomnia, and related dyssomnias, with associated daytime attentional impairment. Co-occurring migraine syndromes and cognitive complaints suggest a neurologic burden linked to disrupted sleep and hypoxia. The profile reflects patients with significant sleep-disordered breathing and its neurocognitive and headache-related sequelae.

phenotypes.label: Poorly controlled diabetes with complications
phenotypes.description: This pattern reflects metabolic syndrome centered on type 2 diabetes with poor glycemic control and established microvascular complications (neuropathy, nephropathy, retinopathy). It co-occurs with hypertension, hyperlipidemia, obesity, and coronary atherosclerosis, indicating high cardiovascular risk. The cluster represents patients with advanced, poorly controlled diabetes and end-organ involvement.

phenotypes.label: Essential tremor
phenotypes.description: This pattern is dominated by tremor disorders, particularly essential tremor, with related coding for tremor syndromes. Other conditions appear incidentally and do not define the clinical picture. The patients represented are characterized primarily by chronic movement disorder with tremor as the central feature.

phenotypes.label: Diabetes and malignancy mix
phenotypes.description: This pattern combines type 1 diabetes mellitus with glycemic instability and complications alongside multiple malignancies, particularly prostate cancer and other neoplastic findings. The endocrine metabolic disease cluster and the cancer-related conditions do not share a single unifying clinical pathway. This reflects a merged grouping of two distinct clinical themes rather than a coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Thromboembolism with infection/autoimmune mix
phenotypes.description: The dominant pattern is venous thromboembolism with embolic events and loss of consciousness, alongside cerebrovascular complications. However, this is intermingled with unrelated genital and respiratory infections and autoimmune/hematologic conditions such as systemic lupus erythematosus and cytopenias. These elements do not form a single coherent clinical syndrome.

phenotypes.label: Psoriasis with psoriatic arthritis
phenotypes.description: This cluster is dominated by psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis, with overwhelming representation of cutaneous and inflammatory joint disease. The surrounding features include other inflammatory dermatoses and musculoskeletal pain syndromes, reflecting the systemic inflammatory burden seen in psoriatic disease. Patients in this group have a clear psoriasis-centered phenotype with frequent joint involvement.

phenotypes.label: Foot disorders and mood
phenotypes.description: This cluster combines podiatric and nail disorders—dystrophic nails, ingrown nails, onychomycosis, toe pain, and callosity—associated with difficulty walking and localized foot symptoms. It also includes episodic mood disorders as a separate prominent component. These represent two unrelated clinical themes (foot pathology with gait impairment and psychiatric illness) without a unifying mechanism.

phenotypes.label: Herpes zoster (shingles)
phenotypes.description: This pattern is overwhelmingly defined by herpes zoster (shingles), with the vast majority of patients carrying this diagnosis. The remaining conditions are sparse and do not form a consistent secondary theme, reflecting incidental comorbidities rather than a unified syndrome.

phenotypes.label: Nonspecific acute symptom cluster
phenotypes.description: This cluster is dominated by nonspecific acute symptoms across multiple systems, including pain, chest pain, shortness of breath, nausea, vomiting, and bowel complaints, with accompanying anxiety. The pattern reflects undifferentiated presentations seen in urgent or general care rather than a single disease process. It captures a broad symptom burden rather than a coherent clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Sleep disorders and hearing loss
phenotypes.description: This grouping combines primary insomnia syndromes with bilateral sensorineural hearing loss and vestibular-type symptoms such as imbalance and nystagmus. These represent distinct clinical domains (sleep disorders versus auditory/vestibular dysfunction) without a single unifying mechanism, suggesting two unrelated condition clusters appearing together.

phenotypes.label: Systemic autoimmune connective tissue disease
phenotypes.description: This cluster represents systemic autoimmune connective tissue diseases, dominated by systemic lupus erythematosus and Sjögren’s syndrome. It includes characteristic multisystem involvement with inflammatory arthritis, interstitial lung disease, Raynaud phenomenon, cytopenias, renal involvement with proteinuria, and cutaneous features such as alopecia and rashes. The pattern reflects patients with systemic autoimmune disease and overlapping organ complications.

phenotypes.label: Liver, skin, and systemic mix
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine nonalcoholic fatty liver disease with dermatologic findings (seborrheic dermatitis, psoriasis, alopecia), flushing, and scattered musculoskeletal and infectious diagnoses. These do not form a single clinical syndrome or shared pathophysiologic pathway. The pattern reflects unrelated liver, skin, and systemic conditions appearing together rather than a coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Severe mental illness with comorbidity
phenotypes.description: This cluster reflects patients with severe mental illness, particularly schizoaffective disorder, accompanied by high rates of nicotine dependence. It is coupled with metabolic disease (severe obesity, dyslipidemia, prediabetes) and chronic infections such as hepatitis C, a pattern consistent with complex behavioral and medical comorbidity. These patients often carry multiple chronic conditions spanning psychiatric, metabolic, and substance-related domains.

phenotypes.label: Mixed infection, endocrine, neurologic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine acute infection (varicella) and atopic tendency with a wide scatter of unrelated endocrine, neurologic, musculoskeletal, and infectious disorders. There is no single organ system or shared mechanism tying these together. This reflects a heterogeneous grouping rather than a clinically coherent syndrome.

phenotypes.label: Borderline personality disorder
phenotypes.description: This pattern is dominated by borderline personality disorder, with the vast majority of patients in this group carrying that diagnosis. The remaining conditions are low-weight, nonspecific comorbid symptoms and chronic conditions that do not define the cluster. It represents patients primarily characterized by borderline personality disorder.
